The **Durham (County Palatine) Act 1836**[[a]](#cite_note-st-1) ([6 & 7 Will. 4](/wiki/6_%26_7_Will._4 "6 & 7 Will. 4"). c. 19) was an [act](/wiki/Act_of_Parliament_(United_Kingdom) "Act of Parliament (United Kingdom)") of the [Parliament of the United Kingdom](/wiki/Parliament_of_the_United_Kingdom "Parliament of the United Kingdom") that abolished the temporal authority of the [Bishop of Durham](/wiki/Bishop_of_Durham "Bishop of Durham") within the [County Palatine of Durham](/wiki/County_Palatine_of_Durham "County Palatine of Durham"), placing the county under lay administration. Previously, since 1075, the so-called [prince-bishops](/wiki/Prince-bishop "Prince-bishop") had substantial powers as [earls](/wiki/Earl "Earl") "with the right to raise an army, mint his own coins, and levy taxes".[[1]](#cite_note-3)[[2]](#cite_note-4)

Section 2 of the act also disbanded the [Court of the County of Durham](/wiki/Court_of_the_County_of_Durham "Court of the County of Durham"), appointing the [High Sheriff](/wiki/High_Sheriff "High Sheriff") as judge of a regular county court.

Doubts about the construction of this act led to the enactment of the [Durham County Palatine Act 1858](/wiki/Durham_County_Palatine_Act_1858 "Durham County Palatine Act 1858") ([21 & 22 Vict.](/wiki/21_%26_22_Vict. "21 & 22 Vict.") c. 45).[[3]](#cite_note-5)

## Repeal

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Durham_(County_Palatine)_Act_1836&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Repeal")]

In a report dated 28 October 1975, the [Law Commission](/wiki/Law_Commission_(England_and_Wales) "Law Commission (England and Wales)") and the [Scottish Law Commission](/wiki/Scottish_Law_Commission "Scottish Law Commission") said that the unrepealed residue of this act was spent because of the abolition of the [Court of Chancery of the County Palatine of Durham and Sadberge](/wiki/Court_of_Chancery_of_the_County_Palatine_of_Durham_and_Sadberge "Court of Chancery of the County Palatine of Durham and Sadberge"). They recommended that the whole act be repealed.[[4]](#cite_note-6) The Act was repealed by [Part I](http://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/1976/16/schedule/1/part/I) of Schedule 1 to the [Statute Law (Repeals) Act 1976](/wiki/Statute_Law_(Repeals)_Act_1976 "Statute Law (Repeals) Act 1976").

## Section 1 – The Palatine Jurisdiction of the Bishop of Durham to be separated from the Bishopric and vested in the Crown

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Durham_(County_Palatine)_Act_1836&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Section 1 – The Palatine Jurisdiction of the Bishop of Durham to be separated from the Bishopric and vested in the Crown")]

The words from "and all forfeitures" to "in right of the same" in this section were repealed by section 9(2) of, and part I of the [third schedule](http://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/Eliz2/9-10/55/schedule/THIRD/enacted) to, the [Crown Estate Act 1961](/wiki/Crown_Estate_Act_1961 "Crown Estate Act 1961").

The proviso to this section was repealed by section 56(4) of, and part II of schedule 11 to, the [Courts Act 1971](/wiki/Courts_Act_1971 "Courts Act 1971"). The repeal was consequential on the abolition of the [Court of Chancery of the County Palatine of Durham and Sadberge](/wiki/Court_of_Chancery_of_the_County_Palatine_of_Durham_and_Sadberge "Court of Chancery of the County Palatine of Durham and Sadberge") by section 41 of that act.

## Section 2 – County court to cease

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Durham_(County_Palatine)_Act_1836&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Section 2 – County court to cease")]

Section 2 of the act abolished the [Court of the County of Durham](/wiki/Court_of_the_County_of_Durham "Court of the County of Durham") and the office of the clerk of that court.

## Section 7 – Extent of the words "County of Durham"

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Durham_(County_Palatine)_Act_1836&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Section 7 – Extent of the words \"County of Durham\"")]

Section 7 of the act provide that:

> That in the Interpretation of the Clauses and Provisions herein-before contained the Words "County of Durham" shall comprise and mean the County of Durham [and Sadberge](/wiki/Sadberge_(wapentake) "Sadberge (wapentake)"), including the detached parts of [Craikshire](/wiki/Craikshire "Craikshire"), [Bedlingtonshire](/wiki/Bedlingtonshire "Bedlingtonshire"), [Norhamshire](/wiki/Norhamshire "Norhamshire"), [Allertonshire](/wiki/Allertonshire "Allertonshire"), and [Islandshire](/wiki/Islandshire "Islandshire"), and all other Places heretofore within the Jurisdiction of the [Bishop of Durham](/wiki/Bishop_of_Durham "Bishop of Durham") in right of the said [County Palatine](/wiki/County_Palatine "County Palatine").

This definition is referred to in section 1 of the [Durham County Palatine Act 1858](/wiki/Durham_County_Palatine_Act_1858 "Durham County Palatine Act 1858") ([21 & 22 Vict.](/wiki/21_%26_22_Vict. "21 & 22 Vict.") c. 45).

## Section 9 – Reservation of rights to the Bishopric

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Durham_(County_Palatine)_Act_1836&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Section 9 – Reservation of rights to the Bishopric")]

Section 9 of the act was repealed by section 9(2) of, and part I of the [third schedule](http://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/Eliz2/9-10/55/schedule/THIRD/enacted) to, the [Crown Estate Act 1961](/wiki/Crown_Estate_Act_1961 "Crown Estate Act 1961").
